project.title: Comparative analysis of intra- and extracellular vesicles from cytotoxic CD8+ αβ T cells and γδ T cells
project.projectDescription: Although belonging to different branches of the immune system, cytotoxic CD8+ αβ T cells and γδ T cells utilize common cytolytic effectors including FasL, granzymes, perforin and granulysin. The effector proteins are stored in different subsets of lysosome-related effector vesicles (LREVs) and released to the immunological synapse upon target cell encounter.  EVs of activated and expanded cytotoxic CD8+ αβ T cells or γδ T cells were enriched from culture supernatants by differential and ultracentrifugation and characterized by a comparative proteomic profiling with tandem mass tags (TMT10plex).
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: EV samples were provided in 200 µL PBS buffer, SDS was added to a final concentration of 1% w/v and the mixture supplemented with cOmplete protease inhibitor cocktail. Samples were lysed in a Bioruptor Pico and BCA analysis performed. Samples were subjected to reduction and alkylation (12 mM Tris(2-carboxyethyl)phosphine, 40 mM 2-chloroacetamide for 1 h at 25 °C) and 10 µg protein removed. SP3 beads were added to each sample (1:40 w/w protein to beads ratio), then ethanol to 50% v/v to induce protein binding (10 min at 25 °C). Beads were immobilized using a magnetic rack, supernatants discarded, and beads washed three times with 80% v/v ethanol. Pellets were resuspended in 50 mM TEAB buffer (pH 8.5) containing 0.2 µg trypsin and incubated for 16 h at 37 °C. Supernatants were subjected to isobaric labelling with TMT10plex reagent; labeling scheme: CD8+ αβ T cells: TMT-126, 127N, 127C, 128N; γδ T cells: TMT-128C, 129N, 129C, 130N. Samples were combined, solid-phase extraction performed and samples dried in a vacuum concentrator. Dried peptides were resuspended in loading buffer (3% ACN/0.1% TFA in water) and subjected to mass spectrometry analysis.  Samples were injected in quadruplicates on a Dionex Ultimate 3000 nano-UHPLC coupled to a Q Exactive HF mass spectrometer. Per injection, 1 µg protein was loaded onto a trap column (Acclaim Pepmap 100 C-18, 5 mm × 300 μm, 5 μm, 100 Å) and washed for 4 min with 3% ACN/0.1% TFA at a flow rate of 30 μl/min prior to peptide separation using an Acclaim PepMap 100 C-18 analytical column (50 cm × 75 μm, 2 μm, 100 Å). A flow rate of 300 nL/min using eluent A (0.05% formic acid (FA)) and eluent B (80% ACN/0.04 % FA) was used for gradient separation (5 - 40% eluent B, 120 min). A spray voltage of 1.8 kV was applied via a liquid junction with a source temperature of 250 °C. Full scan MS spectra were acquired between 300 to 2000 m/z at a resolution of 60,000 at m/z 200, and the top ten most intense precursor ions selected for MS/MS analysis (charge state: ≥+2, isolation window: ±1.2 m/z, HCD fragmentation: 34 NCE). MS/MS spectra were acquired at a resolution of 45,000 with fixed first mass at m/z 100 and dynamic exclusion list of 40 s.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: MS raw files were processed by Proteome Discoverer (version 2.2.0.388) using the Sequest HT algorithm against a human protein database with additional common contaminating proteins (only reviewed sequences) from the UniProt database. Enzymatic processing was set to trypsin (full-specific), maximum missed cleavage events: 2, minimal peptide length: 6 AA, precursor mass tolerance: 10 ppm, fragment mass tolerance: 0.02 Da and false discovery rate set to q = 0.01 by Percolator node. Modifications were set to oxidation on methionine (+15.995 Da, variable), carbamidomethyl on cysteine (+57.021 Da, static) and TMT reagent on lysine and peptide N-termini (+229.163 Da, static). Quantification was performed by a reporter ions quantifier node (integration tolerance: 10 ppm, average reporter S/N threshold: 10).

references.referenceLine: Griesel L, Kaleja P, Tholey A, Lettau M, Janssen O. Comparative Analysis of Extracellular Vesicles from Cytotoxic CD8<sup>+</sup> αβ T Cells and γδ T Cells. Cells. 2024 13(20):1745

Characteristics[CleavageAgent]: trypsin (1:50 enzyme to protein ratio, Promega, Madison, WI, USA)
Characteristics[Label]: isobaric labeled with tandem mass tags (TMT10plex)
labeling scheme: EVs of CD8+ αβ T cells: TMT-126, 127N, 127C, 128N; EVs of γδ T cells: TMT-128C, 129N, 129C, 130N (reagents TMT-130C and 131 were intentionally left unused).
Characteristics[NumberOfBiologicalReplic: for proteome analysis, four individual samples of constitutively released EVs were collected from expanded CD8+ αβ T cells and four samples from zoledronate-expanded γδ T cells.
Characteristics[NumberOfTechnicalReplica: Samples were injected in quadruplicates on a Dionex Ultimate 3000 nano-UHPLC coupled to a Q-Exactive HF mass spectrometer (Thermo Fisher Scientific).
Comment[EnrichmentMethod]: EVs were enriched by differential centrifugation and ultracentrifugation as described before.
Comment[Instrument]: Dionex Ultimate 3000 nano-UHPLC coupled to a Q-Exactive HF mass spectrometer (Thermo Fisher Scientific)
